title: Was vs Were
subtitle: O passado do verbo "to be" — quem usa was, quem usa were

blocks.title: Was
blocks.subtitle: Passado de "to be" para I, he, she, it

examples.pt: Ela estava em casa ontem à noite.

blocks.title: Were
blocks.subtitle: Passado de "to be" para you, we, they

examples.en: We **were** at the beach on Sunday.
examples.pt: Nós estávamos na praia no domingo.

keyPoints: "you" sempre usa were, mesmo no singular

blocks.title: Negativa e Pergunta
blocks.subtitle: Sem auxiliar — o próprio was/were faz tudo

examples.pt: Ele não estava no trabalho ontem.

keyPoints: Não usa did com to be

examples.en: **There was** a problem with my order.
examples.pt: Tinha um problema com o meu pedido.

examples.en: **There were** many people at the show.

blocks.text: Na **2ª condicional**, use **were** para todas as pessoas: `If I were you, I would accept.` (Se eu fosse você...). "If I was you" soa informal e é evitado em textos.
Macete: **was** anda com o singular (I, he, she, it); **were** anda com o resto (you, we, they) — igual à divisão de is/are no presente.

exercises.question: ___ you at the meeting yesterday?
exercises.explanation: Pergunta no passado com to be: **Were** + you. Não use did com to be.

exercises.question: There ___ a lot of traffic on the highway last night.
exercises.explanation: "A lot of traffic" é singular (incontável) → **there was**.

exercises.question: If I ___ you, I wouldn't buy that car.
exercises.explanation: 2ª condicional: **were** para todas as pessoas — `If I were you...`.

exercises.question: She ___ happy with the result, so she complained.
exercises.explanation: She (3ª pessoa singular) no passado, negativa → **wasn't**.

exercises.question: Qual frase está correta?
options: We was tired after the trip.
There were a big party yesterday.
There were two messages for you.
exercises.explanation: Plural (two messages) → **there were**. As outras erram was/were ou usam did com to be.
